title: Sports Network Portal — Club Demo · Full Product Tour

scenes.title: Sports Network Portal
scenes.narration: this is sports network portal. one platform built specifically for sports clubs — academies, federations, multi-sport organizations. web for your staff, mobile for your members. in the next ten minutes, we'll walk through every module so you can decide what to subscribe to. let's start with the problem.
bullets: web for staff · mobile for members

scenes.title: Your club runs on 5 disconnected tools
scenes.narration: your club probably runs on five disconnected tools. whatsapp for announcements, excel for attendance, a separate pos for the store, paper forms for admissions, and someone's personal notes for payroll. every handoff loses data, every parent asks the same question twice, and finance never matches reality. snp replaces all of that with one connected system.

scenes.title: One platform · web + mobile
scenes.narration: here's the model. one platform, two surfaces. the web portal is where staff run the club — coaches, hr, finance, admin. the mobile app is for everyone else — players, parents, members, vip guests. same database, same source of truth. nine modules total, and you only pay for what you turn on.

scenes.title: Subscription · pick your modules
scenes.narration: subscription works like this. you see all nine modules — game management, hr, finance, store, clinic, content hub, announcements, vip club, employees. toggle on what you need, leave the rest off. pricing scales with active members and active modules. start with three, add more as you grow. now let's open the first one.
rows: Game Management · SAR 1,200/mo — Active
HR & Payroll · SAR 1,800/mo — Active
Finance · SAR 1,500/mo — Active
Store & POS · SAR 900/mo — Inactive
VIP Club · SAR 2,400/mo — Trial

scenes.title: Game Management · Overview
scenes.narration: game management is the heart of a sports club. this is the overview. four sports active — football, basketball, tennis, karate. each sport has multi-stage progression, junior moving up to senior. you can see football has four age categories and twelve coaches. the riyadh spring cup is in progress with a budget of eighty-five thousand riyals. one tap and you're inside the tournament.

scenes.title: Admission Review · pending queue
scenes.narration: every new player application lands here. yousef al-mutairi applied for football under-sixteen two days ago. you see his trial score, medical clearance, the fee status — all on one card. approve, reject, or move him to a different age group. no more chasing paper forms between the coach and the front desk.

scenes.title: Applicant History · full timeline
scenes.narration: click into a player and you get the full timeline. yousef's previous trial, last season's stats, his sibling who's already a member, parent contact, payment history. coaches love this because they walk into evaluations already knowing the kid. that's game management. next up — your staff.

scenes.title: HR · Staff · 142 active
scenes.narration: the hr module. one hundred forty-two active staff across all branches. filter by role — coach, trainer, medical, admin. you see ahmed al-mansoori, head football coach at riyadh headquarters. fatima saeed is on six days of medical leave. omar is on notice. every status change flows into payroll automatically.

scenes.title: Payroll · monthly run
scenes.narration: here's payroll for the current month. one hundred forty-two staff, total run of four hundred eighty-six thousand riyals. shift-based coaches get hourly multipliers, full-time staff get their salary plus allowances, and the full-and-final workflow handles exits cleanly with end-of-service gratuity. one click to approve, another to send to finance.

scenes.title: Finance · Cash Flow Command Center
scenes.narration: finance is your command center. revenue this month — six hundred forty-two thousand riyals. outstanding — eighty-nine thousand. overdue bucket — twenty-three thousand, four invoices. the pending queue on the right shows everything waiting for your sign-off — refund requests, the full-and-final from omar, this month's payroll. multi-currency reports if you operate across riyadh, dubai, or beyond.

scenes.narration: drill into transactions. football academy fees just landed — one hundred eighty-four thousand, paid. swimming lessons for under-twelves — twenty-seven thousand, pending. coach payroll for the karate stage is forty-eight thousand and overdue, which is why it's flagged. every line links back to the source — the member, the invoice, the bank reference.

scenes.title: Store · merch + equipment
scenes.narration: the store module. sell jerseys, equipment, branded gear directly to members. inventory is live — three hundred forty-seven units of the home kit, twelve left of the goalkeeper gloves so you reorder. members buy from the mobile app, walk-ins use the front desk pos. every sale hits finance instantly, no double-entry.

scenes.title: Clinic · injuries + medical
scenes.narration: the clinic module. every injury logged, every clearance tracked. fatima the physio sees three players today — a hamstring strain, an ankle rehab on week four, and a pre-season screening. when a player is not cleared, the coach sees it in game management automatically. medical confidentiality is preserved — coaches see status, not diagnosis.

scenes.title: Content Hub · publish to mobile
scenes.narration: the content hub. this is what your members see in the mobile app — match highlights, training tips, club news, coach spotlights. you publish once here, it appears on every member's phone. you can schedule posts, target by age group, and pin announcements. last week's derby highlights reached two thousand four hundred members.

scenes.title: Announcements · time-critical comms
scenes.narration: announcements are different from content. content is engagement — announcements are time-critical. training canceled due to weather. emergency drill at five p.m. tournament bracket released. these go out as push notifications, sms, and email simultaneously, with read receipts so you know who actually got the message.

scenes.title: Employees · self-service hub
scenes.narration: employees is the self-service side of hr. staff log into the portal and request leave, submit expenses, download payslips, see their schedule. ahmed has eighteen leave days remaining. yusuf just submitted a fuel claim for three hundred forty riyals. you approve in two clicks. no more whatsapp screenshots of expense receipts.

scenes.title: VIP Club · premium tier
scenes.narration: the vip club module. this is your premium tier — board members, lifetime members, corporate sponsors. eighty-four vip members right now. they get reserved parking, lounge access, priority booking on courts, and concierge support. each vip has a relationship manager assigned. churn here costs you a lot, so the dashboard shows engagement scores and last-visit dates upfront.

scenes.title: Mobile · the member side
scenes.narration: now flip to mobile. this is what your members see. their schedule, their kid's attendance — eighteen present, two absent today in the under-fourteen squad. invoices, content feed, store, vip lounge. parents stop calling the front desk because everything they need is two taps away. one app, one login, your club in their pocket.

scenes.title: Parent view · child-linked
scenes.narration: for parents, the app is child-linked. one parent can have multiple kids enrolled across different sports. mrs. al-mutairi sees yousef in football under-sixteen and faisal in basketball under-twelve from one screen. payments roll up per family, attendance shows per child, and announcements route to the right parent automatically.

scenes.title: Tournament Bracket · live ops
scenes.narration: back to game management for one more view — live tournament ops. the riyadh spring cup quarter-final, two-one to al-hilal academy in the seventy-third minute. you can see the lineup, substitutions, yellow cards, the next fixture. parents follow on the mobile app, results auto-publish to the content hub, and prize money flows through finance. one module, full event lifecycle.

scenes.title: Multi-currency Reports
scenes.narration: if you operate across borders — riyadh, dubai, doha — finance handles multi-currency natively. revenue rolls up in your base currency, but each branch transacts in local. dubai branch did one hundred twelve thousand dirhams last month, which lands as one hundred fourteen thousand riyals on the consolidated report. fx is automatic, audited, and exportable.
rows: Riyadh HQ — SAR 412,800 · Base
Dubai Branch — AED 112,400 → SAR 114,200
Doha Branch — QAR 68,200 → SAR 70,100
USD Sponsorships — USD 24,500 → SAR 91,800
Consolidated MTD — SAR 688,900

scenes.title: Scheduling · multi-shift
scenes.narration: scheduling deserves its own beat. sports clubs run morning, afternoon, and evening shifts — coaches, lifeguards, front desk, security. snp handles all three with conflict detection. if you double-book coach mohammed for the under-fourteen session and the karate stage at the same time, the system flags it before it goes live. clean rotas, no surprises.
rows: Coach Mohammed · 6am-10am — U-14 Football · OK
Coach Mohammed · 4pm-8pm — Karate Stage · OK
Lifeguard Pool A · 6am-2pm — Yusra · Confirmed
Front Desk · 2pm-10pm — Layla · Confirmed
Security Night · 10pm-6am — Ali · Conflict flag

scenes.title: Member checkout · mobile store
scenes.narration: one last touch on the store. members browse and check out from the phone. apple pay, mada, stc pay, all wired in. order goes to fulfillment, inventory decrements live, finance sees the revenue in real time. average order value at one club we work with is two hundred sixty riyals. that adds up fast across two thousand members.

scenes.title: Multi-tenant · multi-language · multi-currency
scenes.narration: one more thing. snp is multi-tenant out of the box. one parent organization can run multiple branches, multiple sports academies, even franchise locations — each with its own staff, members, books. arabic and english side by side, right-to-left layouts native. saudi riyal, uae dirham, qatari riyal, us dollar. built for the region, ready for the world.

scenes.title: This is how a club becomes a platform
scenes.narration: this is how a club becomes a platform. nine modules, one database, web for your team, mobile for your members. you stop running on five disconnected tools and start running on one. when you're ready, pick the modules you need and we'll have you live in under two weeks. sports network portal. let's build your club into a platform.
